7	package io.javalin.http.servlet
8	
9	import io.javalin.config.JavalinState
10	import io.javalin.http.Context
11	import io.javalin.http.HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
12	import io.javalin.http.HttpStatus.REQUEST_TIMEOUT
13	import io.javalin.http.servlet.SubmitOrder.FIRST
14	import io.javalin.http.servlet.SubmitOrder.LAST
15	import io.javalin.http.util.AsyncUtil.isAsync
16	import io.javalin.http.util.AsyncUtil.newAsyncListener
17	import io.javalin.http.util.ETagGenerator
18	import io.javalin.util.javalinLazy
19	import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet
20	import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest
21	import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse
22	
23	/**
24	 * Handles HTTP requests with sync/async support.
25	 *
26	 * Threading: Sync requests run on the servlet thread. Async requests release the servlet thread
27	 * after ctx.future{} and resume on the future's completion thread. Timeouts fire on Jetty's thread.
28	 * Tasks execute sequentially on one thread at a time; [JavalinServletContext.responseWritten]
29	 * guards against the rare timeout-vs-completion race where both threads try to finalize the response.
30	 */
31	class JavalinServlet(val cfg: JavalinState) : HttpServlet() {
32	
33	    val requestLifecycle = cfg.servletRequestLifecycle.toList()
34	    val router = cfg.internalRouter
35	    private val servletContextConfig by javalinLazy { JavalinServletContextConfig.of(cfg) }
36	
37	    override fun service(request: HttpServletRequest, response: HttpServletResponse) {
38	        handle(JavalinServletRequest(request), response)
39	    }
40	
41	    fun handle(request: HttpServletRequest, response: HttpServletResponse): Context? {
42	        try {
43	            val ctx = JavalinServletContext(
44	                cfg = servletContextConfig,
45	                req = request,
46	                res = response
47	            )
48	
49	            val submitTask: (SubmitOrder, Task) -> Unit = { order, task ->
50	                when (order) {
51	                    FIRST -> ctx.tasks.offerFirst(task)
52	                    LAST -> ctx.tasks.add(task)
53	                }
54	            }
55	            val requestUri = ctx.path().removePrefix(ctx.contextPath())
56	            requestLifecycle.forEach { it.createTasks(submitTask, this, ctx, requestUri) }
57	
58	            ctx.handleSync()
59	            return ctx
60	        } catch (throwable: Throwable) {
61	            router.handleHttpUnexpectedThrowable(response, throwable)
62	            return null
63	        }
64	    }
65	
66	    private fun JavalinServletContext.handleSync() {
67	        while (userFutureSupplier == null && tasks.isNotEmpty()) {
68	            val task = tasks.poll()
69	            if (exceptionOccurred && task.skipOnExceptionAndRedirect) {
70	                continue
71	            }
72	            handleTask(task.handler)
73	        }
74	        when {
75	            userFutureSupplier != null -> handleUserFuture()
76	            else -> writeResponseAndLog()
77	        }
78	    }
79	
80	    private fun JavalinServletContext.handleUserFuture() {
81	        val userFutureSupplier = userFutureSupplier!!.also { userFutureSupplier = null } // nullcheck in handleSync
82	        if (!isAsync()) startAsyncAndAddDefaultTimeoutListeners() // start async if not already started
83	
84	        val userFuture = handleTask { userFutureSupplier.get() } ?: return handleSync() // get future from supplier or handle error
85	        req().asyncContext.addListener(newAsyncListener(onTimeout = { userFuture.cancel(true) })) // cancel user's future if timeout occurs
86	
87	        userFuture
88	            .thenApply { handleSync() }
89	            .exceptionally {
90	                exceptionOccurred = true
91	                router.handleHttpException(this, it)
92	                handleSync() // resume normal request flow (after-handlers, request-logging)
93	            }
94	    }
95	
96	    private fun JavalinServletContext.startAsyncAndAddDefaultTimeoutListeners() = req().startAsync().also {
97	        it.timeout = cfg.http.asyncTimeout
98	        it.addListener(newAsyncListener(onTimeout = { // a timeout avoids the pipeline - we need to handle it manually + it's not thread-safe
99	            status(INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR) // default error handling
100	            router.handleHttpError(statusCode(), this) // user defined error handling
101	            if (resultInputStream() == null) result(REQUEST_TIMEOUT.message) // write default response only if handler didn't do anything
102	            handleSync() // resume normal request flow (after-handlers, request-logging)
103	        }))
104	    }
105	
106	    private fun <R> JavalinServletContext.handleTask(handler: TaskHandler<R>): R? =
107	        try {
108	            handler.handle()
109	        } catch (throwable: Throwable) {
110	            exceptionOccurred = true
111	            userFutureSupplier = null
112	            tasks.offerFirst(Task(skipOnExceptionAndRedirect = false) { router.handleHttpException(this, throwable) })
113	            null
114	        }
115	
116	    private fun JavalinServletContext.writeResponseAndLog() {
117	        try {
118	            if (responseWritten.getAndSet(true)) return // prevent writing more than once, it's required because timeout listener can terminate the flow at any time
119	            resultInputStream()?.use { resultStream ->
120	                val etagWritten = ETagGenerator.tryWriteEtagAndClose(cfg.http.generateEtags, this, resultStream)
121	                if (!etagWritten) resultStream.copyTo(outputStream(), cfg.http.responseBufferSize ?: 32_768) // default should never happen, we add a fallback just in case
122	            }
123	            val executionTime = executionTimeMs()
124	            cfg.httpRequestLoggers.forEach { it.handle(this, executionTime) }
125	        } catch (throwable: Throwable) {
126	            router.handleHttpUnexpectedThrowable(res(), throwable) // handle any unexpected error, e.g. write failure
127	        } finally {
128	            if (outputStreamWrapper.isInitialized()) outputStream().close() // close initialized output wrappers
129	            if (isAsync()) req().asyncContext.complete() // guarantee completion of async context to eliminate the possibility of hanging connections
130	        }
131	    }
132	
133	}
134

33	/**
34	 * Default routing API providing all standard HTTP verbs, lifecycle handlers, and WebSocket support.
35	 * Provides default implementations for convenience methods, requiring implementers to only define:
36	 * - exception(), error(), addEndpoint(), wsException(), addWsHandler()
37	 */
38	interface JavalinDefaultRoutingApi {
39	
40	    /**
41	     * Adds an exception mapper to the instance.
42	     * See: [Exception mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#exception-mapping)
43	     */

45	
46	    /**

48	     * Useful for turning error-codes (404, 500) into standardized messages/pages
49	     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#error-mapping)
50	     */
51	    fun error(status: HttpStatus, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = error(status.code, "*", handler)
52	
53	    /**

57	     */
58	    fun error(status: Int, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = error(status, "*", handler)

60	    /**

63	     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#error-mapping)
64	     */
65	    fun error(status: HttpStatus, contentType: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = error(status.code, contentType, handler)

67	    /**
68	     * Adds an error mapper for the specified content-type to the instance.
69	     * Useful for turning error-codes (404, 500) into standardized messages/pages
70	     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#error-mapping)
71	     */
72	    fun error(status: Int, contentType: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi
73	
74	    /**
75	     * Adds a request handler for the specified handlerType and path to the instance.
76	     * This is the method that all the verb-methods (get/post/put/etc) call.
77	     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
78	     */
79	    fun addHttpHandler(httpMethod: HandlerType, path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(httpMethod, path, handler, *emptyArray())
80	
81	    /**
82	     * Adds a request handler for the specified handlerType and path to the instance.
83	     * This is the method that all the verb-methods (get/post/put/etc) call.
84	     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
85	     */
86	    fun addHttpHandler(handlerType: HandlerType, path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi =
87	        addEndpoint(
88	            Endpoint.create(handlerType, path)
89	                .addMetadata(Roles(roles.toSet()))
90	                .handler(handler)
91	        )
92	
93	    /**
94	     * Adds a request handler for the specified handlerType and path to the instance.
95	     * This is the method that all the verb-methods (get/post/put/etc) call.
96	     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
97	     */
98	    fun addEndpoint(endpoint: Endpoint): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi

package io.javalin.router

import io.javalin.http.ExceptionHandler
import io.javalin.http.Handler
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType

import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.AFTER_MATCHED
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.BEFORE
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.BEFORE_MATCHED
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.DELETE
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.GET
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.HEAD

import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.PATCH
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.POST
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.PUT
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.QUERY
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.WEBSOCKET_AFTER_UPGRADE
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType.WEBSOCKET_BEFORE_UPGRADE

import io.javalin.security.Roles

import io.javalin.websocket.WsConfig

import io.javalin.websocket.WsHandlerType

import io.javalin.websocket.WsHandlerType.WEBSOCKET_AFTER
import io.javalin.websocket.WsHandlerType.WEBSOCKET_BEFORE
import java.util.function.Consumer

/**
 * Default routing API providing all standard HTTP verbs, lifecycle handlers, and WebSocket support.
 * Provides default implementations for convenience methods, requiring implementers to only define:
 * - exception(), error(), addEndpoint(), wsException(), addWsHandler()
 */
interface JavalinDefaultRoutingApi {

    /**
     * Adds an exception mapper to the instance.
     * See: [Exception mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#exception-mapping)
     */
    fun <E : Exception> exception(exceptionClass: Class<E>, exceptionHandler: ExceptionHandler<in E>): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi

    /**
     * Adds an error mapper to the instance.
     * Useful for turning error-codes (404, 500) into standardized messages/pages
     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#error-mapping)
     */
    fun error(status: HttpStatus, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = error(status.code, "*", handler)

    /**
     * Adds an error mapper to the instance.
     * Useful for turning error-codes (404, 500) into standardized messages/pages
     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#error-mapping)
     */
    fun error(status: Int, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = error(status, "*", handler)

    /**
     * Adds an error mapper for the specified content-type to the instance.
     * Useful for turning error-codes (404, 500) into standardized messages/pages
     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#error-mapping)
     */
    fun error(status: HttpStatus, contentType: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = error(status.code, contentType, handler)

    /**
     * Adds an error mapper for the specified content-type to the instance.
     * Useful for turning error-codes (404, 500) into standardized messages/pages
     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#error-mapping)
     */
    fun error(status: Int, contentType: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi

    /**
     * Adds a request handler for the specified handlerType and path to the instance.
     * This is the method that all the verb-methods (get/post/put/etc) call.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun addHttpHandler(httpMethod: HandlerType, path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(httpMethod, path, handler, *emptyArray())

    /**
     * Adds a request handler for the specified handlerType and path to the instance.
     * This is the method that all the verb-methods (get/post/put/etc) call.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun addHttpHandler(handlerType: HandlerType, path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi =
        addEndpoint(
            Endpoint.create(handlerType, path)
                .addMetadata(Roles(roles.toSet()))
                .handler(handler)
        )

    /**
     * Adds a request handler for the specified handlerType and path to the instance.
     * This is the method that all the verb-methods (get/post/put/etc) call.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun addEndpoint(endpoint: Endpoint): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi

    /**
     * Adds a GET request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun get(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(GET, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a POST request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun post(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(POST, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a QUERY request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun query(path: String, handler: Handler) : JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(QUERY, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a PUT request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun put(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(PUT, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a PATCH request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun patch(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(PATCH, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a DELETE request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun delete(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(DELETE, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a HEAD request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun head(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(HEAD, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a OPTIONS request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun options(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(OPTIONS, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a GET request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun get(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(GET, path, handler, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a POST request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun post(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(POST, path, handler, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a QUERY request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun query(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(QUERY, path, handler, *roles)


    /**
     * Adds a PUT request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun put(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(PUT, path, handler, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a PATCH request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun patch(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(PATCH, path, handler, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a DELETE request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun delete(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(DELETE, path, handler, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a HEAD request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun head(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(HEAD, path, handler, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a OPTIONS request handler with the given roles for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#handlers)
     */
    fun options(path: String, handler: Handler, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(OPTIONS, path, handler, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a lambda handler for a Server Sent Event connection on the specified path.
     */
    fun sse(path: String, client: Consumer<SseClient>): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = sse(path, client, *emptyArray())

    /**
     * Adds a lambda handler for a Server Sent Event connection on the specified path.
     */
    fun sse(path: String, handler: SseHandler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = get(path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a lambda handler for a Server Sent Event connection on the specified path.
     */
    fun sse(path: String, client: Consumer<SseClient>, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = get(path, SseHandler(clientConsumer = client), *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a BEFORE request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#before-handlers)
     */
    fun before(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(BEFORE, path, handler)

    /**

    fun before(handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = before("*", handler)

    /**
     * Adds a BEFORE_MATCHED request handler for the specified path to the instance.

     */
    fun beforeMatched(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(BEFORE_MATCHED, path, handler)

    /**

     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#before-handlers)

    fun beforeMatched(handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = beforeMatched("*", handler)

     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#before-handlers)
     */
    fun after(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(AFTER, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds an AFTER request handler for all routes in the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#before-handlers)
     */
    fun after(handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = after("*", handler)

    /**
     * Adds an AFTER_MATCHED request handler for the specified path to the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#before-handlers)
     */
    fun afterMatched(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(AFTER_MATCHED, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds an AFTER_MATCHED request handler for all routes in the instance.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#before-handlers)
     */
    fun afterMatched(handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = afterMatched("*", handler)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket handler of the specified type on the specified path.

     */
    fun addWsHandler(handlerType: WsHandlerType, path: String, wsConfig: Consumer<WsConfig>, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket handler on the specified path.
     * See: [WebSockets in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#websockets)
     */
    fun ws(path: String, ws: Consumer<WsConfig>): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = ws(path, ws, *emptyArray())

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket handler on the specified path with the specified roles.
     * See: [WebSockets in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation#websockets)
     */
    fun ws(path: String, ws: Consumer<WsConfig>, vararg roles: RouteRole): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addWsHandler(WEBSOCKET, path, ws, *roles)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket before handler for the specified path to the instance.
     */
    fun wsBefore(path: String, wsConfig: Consumer<WsConfig>): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addWsHandler(WEBSOCKET_BEFORE, path, wsConfig)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket before handler for all routes in the instance.
     */
    fun wsBefore(wsConfig: Consumer<WsConfig>): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = wsBefore("*", wsConfig)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket before upgrade handler for the specified path to the instance.
     */
    fun wsBeforeUpgrade(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(WEBSOCKET_BEFORE_UPGRADE, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket before upgrade handler for all routes in the instance.
     */
    fun wsBeforeUpgrade(handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = wsBeforeUpgrade("*", handler)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket after handler for the specified path to the instance.
     */
    fun wsAfter(path: String, wsConfig: Consumer<WsConfig>): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addWsHandler(WEBSOCKET_AFTER, path, wsConfig)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket after handler for all routes in the instance.
     */
    fun wsAfter(wsConfig: Consumer<WsConfig>): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = wsAfter("*", wsConfig)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket after upgrade handler for the specified path to the instance.
     */
    fun wsAfterUpgrade(path: String, handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = addHttpHandler(WEBSOCKET_AFTER_UPGRADE, path, handler)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket after upgrade handler for all routes in the instance.
     */
    fun wsAfterUpgrade(handler: Handler): JavalinDefaultRoutingApi = wsAfterUpgrade("*", handler)

package io.javalin.router

import io.javalin.config.JettyConfig
import io.javalin.config.RouterConfig
import io.javalin.event.EventManager
import io.javalin.event.HandlerMetaInfo
import io.javalin.event.WsHandlerMetaInfo
import io.javalin.http.Context
import io.javalin.http.ExceptionHandler
import io.javalin.http.Handler
import io.javalin.http.HandlerType
import io.javalin.router.error.ErrorMapper
import io.javalin.router.exception.ExceptionMapper
import io.javalin.router.matcher.PathMatcher
import io.javalin.security.Roles
import io.javalin.security.RouteRole
import io.javalin.util.Util
import io.javalin.websocket.WsConfig
import io.javalin.websocket.WsExceptionHandler
import io.javalin.websocket.WsHandlerEntry
import io.javalin.websocket.WsHandlerType
import io.javalin.websocket.WsRouter
import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse
import java.util.function.Consumer

open class InternalRouter(
    private val wsRouter: WsRouter,
    private val eventManager: EventManager,
    private val routerConfig: RouterConfig,
    jettyConfig: JettyConfig
) {

    protected open val httpPathMatcher = PathMatcher()
    protected open val httpErrorMapper = ErrorMapper()
    protected open val httpExceptionMapper = ExceptionMapper(routerConfig, jettyConfig)

    /**
     * Adds a request handler for the specified handlerType and path to the instance.
     * This is the method that all the verb-methods (get/post/put/etc) call.
     * See: [Handlers in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation.handlers)
     */
    open fun addHttpEndpoint(endpoint: Endpoint): InternalRouter {
        httpPathMatcher.add(ParsedEndpoint(endpoint, routerConfig))
        eventManager.fireHandlerAddedEvent(
            HandlerMetaInfo(
                httpMethod = endpoint.method,
                path = Util.prefixContextPath(routerConfig.contextPath, endpoint.path),
                handler = endpoint.handler,
                roles = endpoint.metadata(Roles::class.java)?.roles ?: emptySet()
            )
        )
        return this
    }

    /**
     * Get a list of all registered HTTP handlers.
     */
    fun allHttpHandlers(): List<ParsedEndpoint> = httpPathMatcher.allEntries()

    /**
     * Checks if the instance has a handler for the specified handlerType and path.
     */
    open fun hasHttpHandlerEntry(handlerType: HandlerType, requestUri: String): Boolean =
        httpPathMatcher.hasEntries(handlerType, requestUri)

    /**
     * Finds all matching handlers for the specified handlerType and path.
     * @return a handler for the specified handlerType and path, or null if no handler is found
     */
    open fun findHttpHandlerEntries(handlerType: HandlerType, requestUri: String? = null): List<ParsedEndpoint> =
        httpPathMatcher.findEntries(handlerType, requestUri)

    /**
     * Finds the first matching handler for the specified handlerType and path.
     * Short-circuits after the first match, avoiding unnecessary regex checks.
     */
    open fun findFirstHttpHandlerEntry(handlerType: HandlerType, requestUri: String): ParsedEndpoint? =
        httpPathMatcher.findFirstEntry(handlerType, requestUri)

    /**
     * Adds an error mapper for the specified content-type to the instance.
     * Useful for turning error-codes (404, 500) into standardized messages/pages
     * See: [Error mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation.error-mapping)
     */
    open fun addHttpErrorHandler(status: Int, contentType: String, handler: Handler): InternalRouter {
        httpErrorMapper.addHandler(status, contentType, handler)
        return this
    }

    /**
     * Handles an error by looking up the correct error mapper and executing it.
     */
    open fun handleHttpError(statusCode: Int, ctx: Context): Unit =
        httpErrorMapper.handle(statusCode, ctx)

    /**
     * Adds an exception mapper to the instance.
     * See: [Exception mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation.exception-mapping)
     */
    open fun <E : Exception> addHttpExceptionHandler(exceptionClass: Class<E>, exceptionHandler: ExceptionHandler<in E>): InternalRouter {
        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
        httpExceptionMapper.handlers[exceptionClass] = exceptionHandler as ExceptionHandler<Exception>
        return this
    }

    /**
     * Handles an exception by looking up the correct exception mapper and executing it.
     */
    open fun handleHttpException(ctx: Context, throwable: Throwable): Unit =
        httpExceptionMapper.handle(ctx, throwable)

    /**
     * Handles an unexpected throwable by looking up the correct exception mapper and executing it.
     */
    open fun handleHttpUnexpectedThrowable(res: HttpServletResponse, throwable: Throwable): Nothing? =
        httpExceptionMapper.handleUnexpectedThrowable(res, throwable)

    /**
     * Adds a WebSocket exception mapper to the instance.
     * See: [Exception mapping in docs](https://javalin.io/documentation.exception-mapping)
     */
    open fun <E : Exception> addWsExceptionHandler(exceptionClass: Class<E>, exceptionHandler: WsExceptionHandler<in E>): InternalRouter {
        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
        wsRouter.wsExceptionMapper.handlers[exceptionClass] = exceptionHandler as WsExceptionHandler<Exception>
        return this
    }

    /**
     * Adds a specific WebSocket handler for the given path to the instance.
     */
    open fun addWsHandler(handlerType: WsHandlerType, path: String, wsConfig: Consumer<WsConfig>, vararg roles: RouteRole): InternalRouter {
        val roleSet = HashSet(roles.asList())
        wsRouter.addHandler(handlerType, path, wsConfig, roleSet)
        eventManager.fireWsHandlerAddedEvent(
            WsHandlerMetaInfo(
                handlerType = handlerType,
                path = Util.prefixContextPath(routerConfig.contextPath, path),
                wsConfig = wsConfig,
                roles = roleSet
            )
        )
        return this
    }

    /**
     * Get a list of all registered WebSocket handlers.
     */
    fun allWsHandlers(): List<WsHandlerEntry> = wsRouter.wsPathMatcher.allEntries()

}

12	
13	class ErrorMapper {
14	
15	    data class MapperEntry(val statusCode: Int, val contentType: String, val handler: Handler)
16	
17	    private val errorHandlers = mutableSetOf<MapperEntry>()
18	
19	    fun addHandler(statusCode: Int, contentType: String, handler: Handler) {
20	        errorHandlers.add(MapperEntry(statusCode, contentType, handler))
21	    }
22	
23	    fun handle(statusCode: Int, ctx: Context) {
24	        errorHandlers.asSequence()
25	            .filter { it.statusCode == statusCode }
26	            .filter { it.contentType == "*" || ctx.contentTypeMatches(it.contentType) }
27	            .forEach { it.handler.handle(ctx) }
28	    }
29	
30	    private fun Context.contentTypeMatches(contentType: String): Boolean =
31	        header(Header.ACCEPT)?.contains(contentType, ignoreCase = true) == true
32	
33	}

7	package io.javalin.router.exception
8	
9	import io.javalin.config.JettyConfig
10	import io.javalin.config.RouterConfig
11	import io.javalin.http.Context
12	import io.javalin.http.ExceptionHandler
13	import io.javalin.http.HttpResponseException
14	import io.javalin.http.HttpStatus
15	import io.javalin.http.HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
16	import io.javalin.http.InternalServerErrorResponse
17	import io.javalin.util.JavalinLogger
18	import io.javalin.util.Util
19	import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse
20	import java.io.IOException
21	import java.util.concurrent.CompletionException
22	import java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException
23	
24	class ExceptionMapper(private val routerConfig: RouterConfig, private val jettyConfig: JettyConfig) {
25	
26	    val handlers = mutableMapOf<Class<out Exception>, ExceptionHandler<Exception>?>()
27	
28	    init {
29	        handlers[HttpResponseException::class.java] = ExceptionHandler { e, ctx ->
30	            HttpResponseExceptionMapper.handle(e as HttpResponseException, ctx)
31	        }
32	    }
33	
34	    internal fun handle(ctx: Context, t: Throwable) {
35	        if (t is CompletionException && t.cause is Exception) {
36	            return handle(ctx, t.cause as Exception)
37	        }
38	        when {
39	            isClientAbortException(t) -> logDebugAndSetError(t, ctx.res(), HttpStatus.forStatus(jettyConfig.clientAbortStatus))
40	            isJettyTimeoutException(t) -> logDebugAndSetError(t, ctx.res(), HttpStatus.forStatus(jettyConfig.timeoutStatus))
41	            t is Exception -> handleExceptionSafely(ctx, t)
42	            else -> handleUnexpectedThrowable(ctx.res(), t)
43	        }
44	    }
45	
46	    private fun handleExceptionSafely(ctx: Context, exception: Exception) {
47	        try {
48	            Util.findByClass(handlers, exception.javaClass)?.handle(exception, ctx) ?: uncaughtException(ctx, exception)
49	        } catch (exceptionInHandler: Throwable) {
50	            JavalinLogger.warn("Exception handler for %s threw an error".format(exception.javaClass.simpleName), exceptionInHandler)
51	            uncaughtException(ctx, exception)
52	        }
53	    }
54	
55	    private fun uncaughtException(ctx: Context, exception: Exception) {
56	        JavalinLogger.warn("Uncaught exception", exception)
57	        HttpResponseExceptionMapper.handle(InternalServerErrorResponse(), ctx)
58	    }
59	
60	    internal fun handleUnexpectedThrowable(res: HttpServletResponse, throwable: Throwable): Nothing? {
61	        res.status = INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.code
62	        when {
63	            throwable is Error -> routerConfig.javaLangErrorHandler.handle(res, throwable)
64	            isClientAbortException(throwable) -> logDebugAndSetError(throwable, res, HttpStatus.forStatus(jettyConfig.clientAbortStatus))
65	            isJettyTimeoutException(throwable) -> logDebugAndSetError(throwable, res, HttpStatus.forStatus(jettyConfig.timeoutStatus))
66	            else -> JavalinLogger.error("Exception occurred while servicing http-request", throwable)
67	        }
68	        return null
69	    }
70	
71	}
72	
73	private fun unwrap(t: Throwable) = (t as? CompletionException)?.cause ?: t
74	
75	// Jetty throws if client aborts during response writing. testing name avoids hard dependency on jetty.
76	internal fun isClientAbortException(t: Throwable) = unwrap(t)::class.java.name == "org.eclipse.jetty.io.EofException"
77	
78	// Jetty may time out connections to avoid having broken connections that remain open forever
79	// This is rare, but intended (see issues #163 and #1277)
80	private fun isJettyTimeoutException(t: Throwable) = unwrap(t) is IOException && t.cause is TimeoutException
81	
82	private fun logDebugAndSetError(t: Throwable, res: HttpServletResponse, status: HttpStatus) {
83	    JavalinLogger.debug("HTTP " + status.code + " " + status.message, t)
84	    res.status = status.code
85	}

7	package io.javalin.router.matcher
8	
9	import io.javalin.http.HandlerType
10	import io.javalin.router.ParsedEndpoint
11	import java.util.Collections
12	
13	class PathMatcher {
14	
15	    private val handlerEntries: MutableMap<HandlerType, MutableList<ParsedEndpoint>> =
16	        HandlerType.values().associateWithTo(mutableMapOf()) { arrayListOf() }
17	
18	    private fun handlerEntries(handlerType: HandlerType) = handlerEntries[handlerType] ?: emptyList()
19	
20	    fun add(entry: ParsedEndpoint) {
21	        val type = entry.endpoint.method
22	        val path = entry.endpoint.path
23	
24	        handlerEntries.putIfAbsent(type, arrayListOf()) // Ensure the handler type exists in the map (for user-defined http methods)
25	
26	        if (type.isHttpMethod && handlerEntries[type]!!.find { it.endpoint.method == type && it.endpoint.path == path } != null) {
27	            throw IllegalArgumentException("Handler with type='${type}' and path='${path}' already exists.")
28	        }
29	
30	        handlerEntries[type]!!.add(entry)
31	    }
32	
33	    fun findEntries(handlerType: HandlerType, requestUri: String?): List<ParsedEndpoint> {
34	        val candidates = handlerEntries(handlerType)
35	        if (requestUri == null) return Collections.unmodifiableList(candidates)
36	        var firstMatch: ParsedEndpoint? = null
37	        var allMatches: MutableList<ParsedEndpoint>? = null
38	        for (candidate in candidates) {
39	            if (!match(candidate, requestUri)) continue
40	            if (firstMatch == null) {
41	                firstMatch = candidate
42	            } else {
43	                if (allMatches == null) allMatches = ArrayList<ParsedEndpoint>(4).apply { add(firstMatch) }
44	                allMatches.add(candidate)
45	            }
46	        }
47	        return allMatches ?: firstMatch?.let { listOf(it) } ?: emptyList()
48	    }
49	
50	    fun findFirstEntry(handlerType: HandlerType, requestUri: String) =
51	        handlerEntries(handlerType).firstOrNull { match(it, requestUri) }
52	
53	    fun hasEntries(handlerType: HandlerType, requestUri: String) =
54	        findFirstEntry(handlerType, requestUri) != null
55	
56	    fun allEntries() = handlerEntries.values.flatten()
57	
58	    private fun match(entry: ParsedEndpoint, requestPath: String): Boolean = when (entry.endpoint.path) {
59	        "*" -> true
60	        requestPath -> true
61	        else -> entry.matches(requestPath)
62	    }
63	
64	}

11	 */
12	interface EndpointMetadata

19	/**
20	 * Represents an HTTP endpoint in the application.
21	 *
22	 * @param method The HTTP method of the endpoint
23	 * @param path The path of the endpoint
24	 * @param handler The handler of the endpoint
25	 * @param metadata The metadata of the endpoint
26	 */
27	open class Endpoint @JvmOverloads constructor(
28	    @JvmField val method: HandlerType,
29	    @JvmField val path: String,
30	    @JvmField val handler: Handler,
31	    metadata: Set<EndpointMetadata> = emptySet()
32	) {
33	
34	    private val metadata = metadata.associateBy { it::class.java }
35	
36	    /** Execute the endpoint handler with the given executor */
37	    fun handle(executor: EndpointExecutor): Context =
38	        executor.execute(this)
39	
40	    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
41	    fun <METADATA : EndpointMetadata> metadata(key: Class<METADATA>): METADATA? =
42	        metadata[key] as METADATA?
43	
44	    /**
45	     * Creates a copy of this endpoint with additional metadata.
46	     */
47	    fun withMetadata(newMetadata: EndpointMetadata): Endpoint =
48	        Endpoint(method, path, handler, metadata.values.toSet() + newMetadata)
49	
50	    companion object {
51	
52	        class EndpointBuilder internal constructor(val method: HandlerType, val path: String) {
53	
54	            private val metadata = mutableSetOf<EndpointMetadata>()
55	
56	            fun addMetadata(metadata: EndpointMetadata): EndpointBuilder =
57	                apply { this.metadata.add(metadata) }
58	
59	            fun handler(handler: Handler): Endpoint =
60	                Endpoint(method, path, handler, metadata)
61	
62	        }
63	
64	        @JvmStatic
65	        fun create(method: HandlerType, path: String): EndpointBuilder = EndpointBuilder(method, path)
66	    }
67	
68	}
69	
70	/**
71	 * Endpoint executor represents a component that is able to execute an endpoint,
72	 * such as a router or a mock.
73	 */
74	fun interface EndpointExecutor {
75	    fun execute(endpoint: Endpoint): Context
76	}
77	
78	class EndpointNotFound(
79	    method: HandlerType,
80	    path: String
81	) : NotFoundResponse("Endpoint ${method.name()} $path not found")

7	class ParsedEndpoint(

12	    private val pathParser = PathParser(endpoint.path, routerConfig)
13

15	        val pathParams = extractPathParams(requestUri)
16	        val updatedCtx = ctx.update(endpoint, pathParams)
17	        when (val handlerWrapper = routerConfig.handlerWrapper) {
18	            null -> endpoint.handler.handle(updatedCtx)
19	            else -> handlerWrapper.wrap(endpoint).handle(updatedCtx)
20	        }
21	    }
22	
23	    fun matches(requestUri: String): Boolean =
24	        pathParser.matches(requestUri)
25	
26	    fun extractPathParams(requestUri: String): Map<String, String> =
27	        pathParser.extractPathParams(requestUri)
28	
29	}

17	/**

19	 * This is the new preferred way to define routes in Javalin 7.
20	 *
21	 * @param cfg the parent Javalin Configuration
22	 * @see [JavalinState.routes]
23	 */
24	class RoutesConfig(internal val cfg: JavalinState) : JavalinDefaultRoutingApi {
25	
26	    override fun <E : Exception> exception(exceptionClass: Class<E>, exceptionHandler: ExceptionHandler<in E>): RoutesConfig = apply {
27	        cfg.internalRouter.addHttpExceptionHandler(exceptionClass, exceptionHandler)
28	    }
29	
30	    override fun error(status: Int, contentType: String, handler: Handler): RoutesConfig = apply {
31	        cfg.internalRouter.addHttpErrorHandler(status, contentType, handler)
32	    }
33	
34	    override fun addEndpoint(endpoint: Endpoint): RoutesConfig = apply {
35	        cfg.internalRouter.addHttpEndpoint(endpoint)
36	    }
37	
38	    override fun <E : Exception> wsException(exceptionClass: Class<E>, exceptionHandler: WsExceptionHandler<in E>): RoutesConfig = apply {
39	        cfg.internalRouter.addWsExceptionHandler(exceptionClass, exceptionHandler)
40	    }
41	
42	    override fun addWsHandler(handlerType: WsHandlerType, path: String, wsConfig: Consumer<WsConfig>, vararg roles: RouteRole): RoutesConfig = apply {
43	        cfg.internalRouter.addWsHandler(handlerType, path, wsConfig, *roles)
44	    }
45	
46	    /**
47	     * Adds routes using the ApiBuilder DSL.
48	     * This method allows you to define routes using the static methods from ApiBuilder.
49	     *
50	     * @param endpoints the endpoint group containing the route definitions
51	     * @return this RoutesConfig instance for method chaining
52	     */
53	    fun apiBuilder(endpoints: EndpointGroup): RoutesConfig = apply {
54	        try {
55	            ApiBuilder.setStaticJavalin(this)
56	            endpoints.addEndpoints()
57	        } finally {
58	            ApiBuilder.clearStaticJavalin()
59	        }
60	    }

package io.javalin.http.servlet

import io.javalin.compression.CompressedOutputStream

import java.nio.charset.Charset
import java.util.*
import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture
import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean
import java.util.function.Supplier
import java.util.stream.Stream
import kotlin.LazyThreadSafetyMode.PUBLICATION
import kotlin.LazyThreadSafetyMode.SYNCHRONIZED

data class JavalinServletContextConfig(
    val appDataManager: AppDataManager,
    val pluginManager: PluginManager,
    val compressionStrategy: CompressionStrategy,
    val requestLoggerEnabled: Boolean,
    val defaultContentType: String,
    val jsonMapper: JsonMapper,
    val strictContentTypes: Boolean,
    val multipartConfig: MultipartConfig
) {
    companion object {
        fun of(cfg: JavalinState): JavalinServletContextConfig =
            JavalinServletContextConfig(
                appDataManager = cfg.appDataManager,
                pluginManager = cfg.pluginManager,
                compressionStrategy = cfg.http.compressionStrategy,
                requestLoggerEnabled = cfg.httpRequestLoggers.isNotEmpty(),
                defaultContentType = cfg.http.defaultContentType,
                jsonMapper = cfg.jsonMapper.value,
                strictContentTypes = cfg.http.strictContentTypes,
                multipartConfig = cfg.jetty.multipartConfig,
            )
    }
}

open class JavalinServletContext(
    private val cfg: JavalinServletContextConfig,
    val tasks: Deque<Task> = ArrayDeque(8),
    var exceptionOccurred: Boolean = false,
    val responseWritten: AtomicBoolean = AtomicBoolean(false),
    private var req: HttpServletRequest,
    private val res: HttpServletResponse,
    private val startTimeNanos: Long? = if (cfg.requestLoggerEnabled) System.nanoTime() else null,
    private var routeRoles: Set<RouteRole> = emptySet(),
    internal var userFutureSupplier: Supplier<out CompletableFuture<*>>? = null,
    private var resultStream: InputStream? = null,
    private var minSizeForCompression: Int = cfg.compressionStrategy.defaultMinSizeForCompression,
) : Context {

    private val endpoints: Endpoints = Endpoints()

    // State: 0 = UNSET, 1 = TRUE, -1 = FALSE
    private var cachedWillMatchState: Int = 0

    @JvmSynthetic
    internal inline fun cachedWillMatch(lookup: () -> Boolean): Boolean {
        if (cachedWillMatchState == 0) {
            cachedWillMatchState = if (lookup()) 1 else -1
        }
        return cachedWillMatchState == 1
    }

    private var cachedHttpHandlerValue: Any? = UNSET

    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
    internal inline fun cachedHttpHandler(lookup: () -> ParsedEndpoint?): ParsedEndpoint? {
